text: 30. ARBITRATION: All controversies, claims or disputes between the Contractor and the Subcontractor arising out of, or relating to,  the  sublet  work,  this  subcontract  or  the  breach  thereof  shall  be  settled  by  arbitration  in  accordance  with  the  construction industry  arbitration  rules  of  the  American  Arbitration  Association  then  effective,  unless  the  parties  mutually  agree  otherwise. Notice  of  the  demand  of  arbitration  shall  be  filed  in  writing  with  the  other  party  to  the  subcontract  and  with  the  American Arbitration Association and shall be made within a reasonable time after the dispute has arisen, not to exceed the period of any otherwise applicable statute of limitations. The award rendered by the arbitrators shall be final and binding, and judgment may be entered upon it in accordance with applicable law in any court having jurisdiction thereof. No arbitration rising out of or relating to this subcontract shall include, by consolidation, joinder or any other manner, any person or entity not a party to the agreement under which such arbitration arises, unless it is shown at the time the demand for arbitration is filed that (1) such person or entity is substantially involved in a common question of fact or law, or (2) the presence of such person or entity is required if complete relief is to be accorded in the arbitration, and the person or entity sought to be joined has given their written consent to be joined. The agreement herein among the parties to the agreement and any other written agreement to arbitrate referred to herein shall be specifically enforceable under the Federal Arbitration Act. Notwithstanding the foregoing or anything herein to the contrary, if Contractor is a party to a litigation or arbitration proceeding with the Owner, all or part of which arises out of or relates to the work, the performance of the work, or this subcontract, Subcontractor shall, upon Contractor's written request, join as a party to such  arbitration  or  litigation  proceeding.  The  Contractor  shall  be  fully  compensated  for  the  cost  of  its  in  pursuing  all controversies, claims, or disputes against Subcontractor, including the reasonable cost incurred for attorneys', consultants', and experts' fees.
